sglang.title: [FEAT] Add transformers backend support

What does this PR do ?
Fixes #5471
This PR adds support for transformers as a backend
Thanks @Isotr0py and @XuehaiPan for the help !
Additional features added:

 TP
 trust_remote_code=True
 quantization (tested with torchao and tp_size =2)
 LORAs (maybe in a follow-up PR)

Examples

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Use cmake 3.26.1 instead of 3.26 to avoid build failure
vllm.author: houseroad
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
To address #18748
Test plan:
Verified with cmake 3.26.0 (problematic), 3.26.1, 3.27.9, 3.30.5, 4.0.2. All others are good, only 3.26.0 only generate _C.so, not _C.abi3.so.
python setup.py develop with cmake==3.26.0
-- Install configuration: "RelWithDebInfo"
-- Installing: /home/lufang/gitrepos/vllm/

vllm.title: [v1][KVCacheManager] Rename BlockHashType to BlockHash
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We usually don't use XXXType as a class name, so I suggest to change BlockHashType to BlockHash.

vllm.title: Update docker docs with ARM CUDA cross-compile
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
I ran into this complication when trying to cross-compile ARM wheels on my x86 CUDA machine. After running this extra command, the following command works
DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build . \                       
  --file docker/Dockerfile \
  --target build \
  --platform "linux/arm64" \
  -t vllm/

Transformers v4.52 will map weights name for Qwen2-VL/Qwen2.5-VL: https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/de4cf5a38e9678b9e465867a8a6b88ea727bea52/src/transformers/models/qwen2_vl/modeling_qwen2_vl.py#L1359-L1362
So the qwen2-vl/qwen2.5-vl checkpoints saved after transformers v4

Essential Elements of an Effective PR Description Checklist

 The purpose of the PR, such as "Fix some issue (link existing issues this PR will resolve)".
 The test plan, such as providing test command.
 The test results, such as pasting the results comparison before and after, or e2e results

vllm.title: [V1] Support cross-layer KV sharing
vllm.author: shinoss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Some models like Tencent-Hunyuan-Large (#10043) and Hymba-1.5B-Base (#10783) use cross-layer KV sharing (e.g. Cross-Layer Attention). This PR adds the ability for KV caches to be shared between attention layers.
Design
This PR adds a new argument kv_sharing_target_layer_name: Optional[str

vllm.title: [Perf] Tune `scaled_fp8_quant` by increasing vectorization
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Increase the vectorization from 4 to 16 elements and decrease the block_size to launch for kernel for the per-tensor and per-token fp8 quantization CUDA kernels. The improvements are visible on H100 and obvious on B200.
Evaluations
# Command for static per-tensor quantization
lm_eval --model vllm --

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Handle nested Annotation and Optional type hints. Before this PR, only one would be handled for a given type hint

vllm.title: [v1] Re-init input batch for multiple kv cache groups
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Ideally, we should initialize the input batch inside initialize_kv_cache based on the kv cache config. However, as in #18298, due to some unknown reasons, we have to initialize the input batch before load_model, quantization + weight offloading will fail otherwise.
As a temporary solution, we initia

vllm.title: [V1][Spec Decode][Ngram] 1.35x gain -> 1.95x gain on InstructCoder with prompt fix
vllm.author: ekagra-ranjan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
So far we only have InstructCoder #15303 as the only dataset where we are able to benchmark a realistic editing task where ngram shines. Earlier, we only got 1.35x gain even though the ngram match were supposed to be high since we are editiing code (see the dataset).
On investigation, I found that t

vllm.title: [Bugfix] get_num_blocks_to_allocate with null_block
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
null_block should not be counted as num_evictable_computed_blocks.
This PR also changes null block check from block != self.null_block to block.is_not_null as suggested by #18652

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Max concurrency estimation and check_enough_kv_cache_memory for models with sliding window layers
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes two bugs:


If a model contains both sliding window attention and full attention, the sliding window attention layers are regarded as full attention layers when allocating kv cache, but in check_enough_kv_cache_memory, they are still regarded as sliding window attention layers. This PR

vllm.title: feat: add data parallel rank to KVEventBatch
vllm.author: PeaBrane
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Overview:

Add data_parallel_rank to KvEventBatch, so it can be published along with the block storage / removal events
Perform a port offset based on the dp rank for EventPublisher so there is no port conflict
Allow the mock subscriber to subscribe to multiple ports for testing
Added a test for tes

change python -> python3. this file has both python and python3 in use. I just change to python3 for consistency.  some system like vllm official container doesn't have python alias.

vllm.title: [Docs] Add developer doc about CI failures
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This document describes what to do when CI fails on a PR and appears to
be unrelated to the changes in the PR. We have a GitHub dashboard of
open issues related to failing tests. This doc encourages more people to
help file and fix these issues.
Co-authored-by: Mark McLoughlin markmc@redhat.com
Sign

vllm.author: bigPYJ1151
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
resolve #16056
Support all features listed in the CPU doc excepts FP8 KV cache.
Changes

Add V1 CPUWorker and CPUModelRunner, derived from Worker and GPUModelRunner to reduce code duplication.
Add V1 TorchSDPABackend with compatible interfaces for GPUModelRunner, such as reorder_batch and build.
Add

vllm.title: [Core] Cast multimodal input in hf processor
vllm.author: lgeiger
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a follow up to #18756 and instead directly does the multimodal input casting as part of the huggingface preprocessing.
I think this is a bit cleaner and has two advantages: It moves the blocking casting/copy off the main thread and now does the conversion before serialisation which also redu

vllm.title: [KERNEL] Sampler. CUDA kernel for applying repetition penalty
vllm.author: vadiklyutiy
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem
Sampler's part that responsible for applying repetition penalty took a long time, especially for small models.
Solution
This PR introduce a CUDA kernel that implements applying repetition penalty
Performance
All measurements on H100
Unit test
Tests of "torch implementation" vs "new CUDA kern

Exec the example will result in the following error because, in v1, the Request-level structured output does not support the guided_decoding_backend parameter.
https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/v1/engine/processor.py#L161

vllm.title: [NVIDIA] Add Cutlass MLA backend
vllm.author: kaixih
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR introduces the CUTLASS_MLA_VLLM_V1 backend, enabling support for ops.cutlass_mla_decode() on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs.
It also includes performance results using DeepSeek-V3 on 8×B200 GPUs under DP+EP parallelism settings, which delivers ~17% improved throughput.
